report_name: Woori Bank Data Leak: Third-Party NFT Developer Exposes Data of 17,551 Customers

threat_actors.name: Third-party NFT Platform Developer Employee
threat_actors.description: An employee at a third-party software development firm contracted by Woori Bank, who improperly retained and exposed customer data on a public developer platform after a project's completion due to a failure in process and oversight.
threat_actors.goals: The developer's action of uploading data was not described with a specific goal; it was a result of improper data handling.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: Woori Bank's data was exposed by a third-party software developer contracted to build an NFT platform, representing a breach originating from a trusted partner.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The third-party developer failed to delete customer data from the project environment after completion, instead retaining it improperly.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The improperly retained customer data was uploaded to a public developer platform, making it publicly accessible.

identities.name: Woori Bank
identities.description: A major South Korean financial institution that suffered a data leak affecting 17,551 customers due to mishandling by a third-party developer.

identities.name: Woori Bank Customers
identities.description: A group of 17,551 customers of Woori Bank whose nicknames and encrypted 'connecting information' (CI) identifiers were exposed.

infrastructure.name: Public Developer Platform
infrastructure.description: A public platform for developers where customer data from Woori Bank was uploaded and exposed.

indicators.name: Anomalous Large Data Upload to Developer Platform
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Large Data Upload to Developer Platform
id: 2b1c8e9f-a3d4-4b1c-8e9f-a3d4b1c8e9fa
status: experimental
description: Detects large data uploads to common developer platforms, which could indicate data exfiltration, such as the incident where a developer exposed Woori Bank customer data.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-05
references:
  - https://www.koreaherald.com/article/10798402
tags:
  - attack.t1567.002
  - attack.exfiltration
logsource:
  category: proxy
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate large code, data, or artifact pushes by developers.
level: medium
detection:
  selection_hosts:
    cs-host|endswith:
      - '.github.com'
      - '.gitlab.com'
      - '.bitbucket.org'
  selection_method:
    cs-method: 'POST'
  selection_size:
    sc-bytes: '>10000000'
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: This behavioral rule detects large data uploads (over 10MB) to common public developer platforms. This could be an indicator of data exfiltration similar to the method used in the Woori Bank data leak.

relationships.source_name: Anomalous Large Data Upload to Developer Platform
